section: Chapter 2: Is Matter Around Us Pure?

questions.question: What is a pure substance in scientific terms?
questions.correct_answer: A substance where all constituent particles are the same in their chemical nature
questions.explanation: A pure substance consists of a single type of particle.

questions.question: What is a mixture?
questions.correct_answer: Matter constituted by more than one kind of pure form of matter
questions.explanation: Mixtures can be separated into their pure components by physical processes.

questions.question: Define a homogeneous mixture.
questions.correct_answer: A mixture that has a uniform composition throughout
questions.explanation: Examples include salt dissolved in water or sugar in water.

questions.question: Define a heterogeneous mixture.
questions.correct_answer: A mixture that contains physically distinct parts and non-uniform compositions
questions.explanation: Examples include mixtures of sodium chloride and iron filings, or oil and water.

questions.question: What are the two components of a solution?
questions.explanation: The solvent is the component in larger amount; the solute is the component being dissolved.

questions.question: What is a saturated solution?
questions.correct_answer: A solution in which no more solute can be dissolved at a particular temperature
questions.explanation: At any particular temperature, a solution that has dissolved as much solute as it is capable of dissolving is said to be saturated.

questions.question: What is a suspension?
questions.correct_answer: A heterogeneous mixture in which the solute particles do not dissolve but remain suspended
questions.explanation: Particles of a suspension are visible to the naked eye and scatter light.

questions.question: What is a colloid?
questions.correct_answer: A mixture where particle size is intermediate between a true solution and a suspension
questions.explanation: Colloids appear homogeneous but are actually heterogeneous; they are stable and scatter light.

questions.question: What is the Tyndall effect?
questions.correct_answer: The scattering of a beam of light by colloidal particles
questions.explanation: This scattering makes the path of light visible.

questions.question: Differentiate between an element and a compound.
questions.correct_answer: An element is a basic form of matter that cannot be broken down; a compound is composed of two or more elements chemically combined
questions.explanation: Elements are the simplest form; compounds have fixed proportions and different properties from their constituents.

notes.title: Properties of Solutions
notes.content: Solutions are homogeneous, stable, have particles smaller than 1nm, and do not show Tyndall effect.

notes.title: Colloid Classification
notes.content: Classified by the state of dispersing medium and dispersed phase (e.g., Aerosol, Foam, Emulsion).

notes.title: Physical vs Chemical Change
notes.content: Physical changes involve state changes without new substances; chemical changes result in new substances.

facts.fact: Alloys are homogeneous mixtures of metals that cannot be separated by physical methods.
Tincture of iodine is a solution of iodine (solute) in alcohol (solvent).
Mercury is the only metal that is liquid at room temperature.
